description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, giving it a classic and sturdy appearance. The front panel features a large black speaker grille on the left, three cream-colored rotary knobs for tuning and volume control, and a frequency display window with AM and FM bands clearly marked. The top, sides, and back are finished in a rich, woodgrain texture, while the back panel includes several circular ports and a recessed area, likely for power and antenna connections. The overall design is both functional and decorative, suitable for placement on a table, shelf, or countertop in an urban environment. The materials and build suggest durability and a tactile, retro aesthetic.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a black speaker grille, three cream knobs, and a frequency display on the front. The body is finished in woodgrain with rounded edges and a sturdy, classic look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black speaker grille, three cream rotary knobs, and the frequency display window.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, continuous woodgrain surface with a single knob protruding slightly.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a black recessed panel with three circular ports and a large rectangular area, likely for connections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the uninterrupted woodgrain surface with no visible controls or features.
